\chapter{Concordance with primary literature}
\label{chap:concordance}

\index{concordance|textbf}

This chapter is the constitution of the monograph. When chapters
disagree, this chapter governs. It records the five main theorems
with their precise status and locates every result against the
prior literature: what is proved, what is conditional, what
remains conjectural.

The governing principle is falsification: every claim in these three
volumes is presumed false until it appears below with an explicit
status tag and a proof reference or independent verification trail.
A theorem that contradicts the concordance is not a theorem; it is an
error in the chapter that states it. Omission from this chapter is
itself a verdict: what is not recorded here has not survived scrutiny.
The concordance does not summarize the monograph; the monograph
approximates the concordance.

# CLAUDE.md -- Modular Koszul Duality Programme (Canonical Reference)

## Identity

E_1-E_1 operadic Koszul duality in the homotopical modular chiral realm on algebraic curves. One form (eta = d log(z_1 - z_2)), one relation (Arnold), one object (Theta_A), one equation (D*Theta + 1/2[Theta,Theta] = 0). The primitive object is B^ord(A) = T^c(s^{-1}A-bar): ordered bar, deconcatenation coproduct, R-matrix, Yangian. The symmetric bar B^Sigma is the Sigma_n-coinvariant shadow. Physics IS the homotopy type: A-infinity = scattering, SC^{ch,top} governs the (bulk, boundary) pair, modular L-infinity = genus tower. The five theorems A-D+H are the invariants that survive averaging.

**Bar complex is E_1-coassociative; SC^{ch,top} emerges on the derived center (CRITICAL, corrected 2026-04-12):** The bar complex B^{ord}(A) = T^c(s^{-1}A-bar) is an E_1-chiral coassociative COALGEBRA (over ChirAss^!). It has a differential + deconcatenation coproduct. It does NOT carry SC^{ch,top} structure. The SC^{ch,top} structure (or E_3 with conformal vector) emerges on the DERIVED CENTER Z^{der}_{ch}(A) = ChirHoch*(A,A), computed USING the bar complex as a resolution. The bar complex is the E_1 engine; the derived center is the SC^{ch,top}/E_3 output. thm:bar-swiss-cheese (claiming B(A) is an SC-coalgebra) needs retraction or careful restatement — see FOUND-11. princ:sc-two-incarnations in en_koszul_duality.tex states this correctly.

Three volumes by Raeez Lorgat. Vol I *Modular Koszul Duality* (this repo, ~2,650pp). Vol II *A-infinity Chiral Algebras and 3D HT QFT* (~/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2, ~1,704pp). Vol III *CY Categories, Quantum Groups, and BPS Algebras* (~/calabi-yau-quantum-groups, ~259pp). Total ~4,613pp, 120K+ tests, 3,463 tagged claims. This file is the canonical reference; Vols II/III inherit shared content and add volume-specific material.

**Crystallized programme identity (2026-04-12):** We are studying **holomorphic chiral (factorisation) (co)homology** via **bar and cobar chain constructions** at **various different geometric locations**, hence the **different (modular) operads** at play. The geometry determines the operad, the operad determines the bar complex, the bar complex computes the factorisation (co)homology. The five theorems are structural properties. The shadow tower is the characteristic class data. The E_n circle is the holographic structure.

**The E_n operadic circle (2026-04-12):** E_3(bulk) → E_2(boundary chiral) → E_1(bar/QG) → E_2(Drinfeld center) → E_3(derived center). Each arrow is: restriction to codim-2 defect, ordered bar complex, categorified averaging (Drinfeld center), higher Deligne (derived center). The circle closes for 3d HT theories with conformal vector; without conformal vector, stuck at SC^{ch,top} (the intermediary between E_1-chiral and E_3).

**SC^{ch,top} ≠ E_3 (2026-04-12):** The Swiss-cheese operad is two-coloured with directionality (no open-to-closed). Dunn additivity does NOT apply. E_3 requires the topologization theorem: SC^{ch,top} + inner conformal vector (Sugawara at non-critical level, making C-translations Q-exact) = E_3. Without conformal vector: stuck at SC^{ch,top}. thm:topologization in en_koszul_duality.tex PROVED for affine KM V_k(g) at non-critical level k != -h^v (Sugawara explicit). For general chiral algebras with conformal vector (Virasoro, W-algebras): CONJECTURAL (conj:topologization-general), depends on constructing the 3d HT BRST complex. Proof is cohomological (Q-cohomology); for class M, chain-level E_3 may fail.

**Five notions of E_1-chiral algebra (2026-04-12):** (A) strict ChirAss-algebra, (B) A_inf in End^{ch}_A, (C) EK quantum vertex algebra, (D) A_inf in E_1-chiral, (E) factorization on Ran^{ord}(X). Each has own derived center. (B)↔(C) via Drinfeld associator on Koszul locus. Warning installed at algebraic_foundations.tex warn:multiple-e1-chiral.

**Three Hochschild theories (2026-04-12):** (i) Topological HH: E_1-algebra input → E_2 output (Deligne). (ii) Chiral HH (ChirHoch): E_inf-chiral input → E_inf output, concentrated {0,1,2} (Theorem H). (iii) Categorical HH: dg category input → E_2 with CY shifted Poisson. NEVER conflate. The geometry determines which Hochschild: curve X → chiral, R → topological, CY category → categorical. Constitutional statement in memory/project_hochschild_constitutional_statement.md.

name: claim-surface-sync
description: Use when theorem labels, status tags, concordance text, theorem registry entries, metadata, or duplicated theorem surfaces may drift out of sync. Not for purely local wording edits that do not affect epistemic status.
---

# Claim-Surface Sync

Use this skill when a correction is not finished until the surrounding status surface agrees with it.

## Load first

- `chapters/connections/concordance.tex`
- `metadata/theorem_registry.md`
- the relevant metadata files under `metadata/`
- the target `.tex` files
- any duplicated or cross-volume occurrences if available

## Sync protocol

1. Identify the canonical claim surface:

import re, subprocess, os
labels = subprocess.check_output(\"(grep -roh '\\\\\\\\label{["'^}]*}'"' ~/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/ ~/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2/chapters/ ~/calabi-yau-quantum-groups/chapters/ 2>/dev/null) | sort | uniq -d | sed -n '1,20p'\", shell=True, text=True)
labels = [re.search(r'\\\\label\\{(["'^}]*)'"\\}', line).group(1) for line in labels.splitlines() if line.strip()]
for lab in labels:
    print(f'LABEL {lab}')
    cmd = f\"rg -n --glob '*.tex' '\\\\\\\\label\\\\{{{re.escape(lab)}\\\\}}|\\\\\\\\ref\\\\{{{re.escape(lab)}\\\\}}|\\\\\\\\eqref\\\\{{{re.escape(lab)}\\\\}}|\\\\\\\\Cref\\\\{{{re.escape(lab)}\\\\}}|\\\\\\\\autoref\\\\{{{re.escape(lab)}\\\\}}' ~/chiral-bar-cobar ~/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2 ~/calabi-yau-quantum-groups\"
    try:
        out = subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        out = e.output
    print(out)

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import re, subprocess, shlex
cmd = \"(grep -roh '\\\\\\\\label{["'^}]*}'"' ~/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/ ~/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2/chapters/ ~/calabi-yau-quantum-groups/chapters/ 2>/dev/null) | sort | uniq -d | sed -n '1,20p'\"
labels = [re.search(r'\\\\label\\{(["'^}]*)'"\\}', line).group(1) for line in subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True).splitlines()]
roots = [('/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters','v1'),('/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2/chapters','v2'),('/Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups/chapters','v3')]
for lab in labels:
    print(f'LABEL {lab}')
    pattern = '\\\\label{' + lab + '}'
    for root, vol in roots:
        fcmd = f\"find {shlex.quote(root)} -type f -print0 | xargs -0 grep -Hn {shlex.quote(pattern)} 2>/dev/null\"
        try:
            out = subprocess.check_output(fcmd, shell=True, text=True)
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            out = e.output
        if out.strip():
            print(f'  {vol}:')
            for line in out.strip().splitlines():
                print('   ', line)
    print()

\begin{theorem}[Bershadsky--Polyakov is strict in canonical normal form; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{thm:bp-strict}
For every $k\neq -3$, the canonical normalized completed dual of the
Bershadsky--Polyakov algebra $\mathcal W_3^{(2)}(k)$ has
\[
d_r=0 \qquad (r\geq 3).
\]
Equivalently, its canonical homotopy degree is $2$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
The singular OPE coefficients in
\eqref{eq:bp-ef}--\eqref{eq:bp-heis} have generator degree at most $2$.

\bigskip

\noindent\textbf{Final principle.}
The diagonal is the boundary. The bar coalgebra is the ambient ordered phase space. The annulus is
the self-cotensor of the diagonal. Once this is recognized, the associative chiral duality ceases
to be merely an equivalence between algebra and coalgebra and becomes a machine for open traces,
centers, and eventually bordered genus.

(Theorem~\ref{thm:dirichlet-weight-formula})
factors the Heisenberg sewing determinant over primes
via the unordered divisor function $\sigma_{-1}$.

Part~I develops the categorical logarithm in full generality,
Part~II extracts its nonlinear content through the shadow obstruction tower,
Part~III develops the ordered ($E_1$) refinement,
Part~IV carries the engine out for every major family, and Part~V
connects them to BV-BRST quantization, Feynman integrals, and
holomorphic-topological field theory.

The bar complex looks like a Feynman expansion. The differential
is a sum over trees weighted by propagators $\eta_{ij} = d\log(z_i - z_j)$,
the degree counts vertices, and iterated residues on
Fulton--MacPherson boundary strata play the role of UV divergences.
The similarity is striking enough to demand an answer: is the bar
complex a literal reformulation of perturbative QFT, or only a
structural analogy?

The honest answer distinguishes two regimes. For the Heisenberg
algebra $\mathcal{H}_{\kappa^{\mathrm{Heis}}}$, the bar complex
reproduces the free-boson residue calculus exactly, and
Conjecture~\ref{conj:bar-cobar-path-integral-heisenberg} records the
expected all-genera path-integral identification. For interacting
theories, Volume~I proves only the
genus-$0$ algebraic BRST/bar comparison on the Kac--Moody locus and
the local binary/ternary reduction package; the general perturbative
comparison remains conjectural
\textup{(}Conjecture~\ref{conj:bar-cobar-path-integral}\textup{)}.
The Feynman--bar mnemonic (Remark~\ref{rem:feynman-bar-bridge})

\chapter{BV-BRST formalism and Gaiotto's perspective}
\label{ch:bv-brst}
\index{Batalin--Vilkovisky|see{BV algebra}}

The physicist's BRST cohomology and the algebraist's bar resolution
are not analogous: they are the same computation. The BV formalism
is a machine for computing derived functors; the bar complex is a
machine for computing derived functors; at genus~$0$, they are the
same machine applied to the same input. Costello's formulation of
perturbative QFT as the theory of derived moduli problems makes the
identification inevitable: the BRST operator computes the derived
space of gauge-equivalence classes, which is what the bar differential
computes on the algebraic side. What requires proof is not the
coincidence but the precise scope of the agreement.

The BV Laplacian and the sewing operator agree at genus~$0$. The
quantum master equation $\hbar \Delta S + \tfrac12\{S,S\} = 0$ of
the BV formalism coincides on $\mathbb{P}^1$ with the bar
 succeeded in 51ms:

\chapter{Feynman diagram interpretation of bar-cobar duality}
\label{ch:feynman}

\begin{remark}[Heuristic Feynman--bar mnemonic; \ClaimStatusHeuristic]
\label{rem:feynman-bar-bridge}
\index{Feynman diagram!bar-cobar bridge|textbf}
Let $\cA$ be a chiral algebra on a smooth curve~$X$ with OPE
$a(z)\,b(w) \sim \sum_n C^{ab}_n(w)\,(z-w)^{-n-1}$.
The bar complex carries the collision data that a perturbative
Feynman-style reading probes.
\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.25}
\begin{tabular}{lcl}
\textbf{Perturbative QFT} && \textbf{Bar complex $\barBch(\cA)$} \\
\hline
propagator $G(z_i,z_j) = (z_i-z_j)^{-1}$
 && $d\log$ kernel $\eta_{ij} = d\log(z_i - z_j)$ \\
vertex factor $C^{ab}_n$

The algebraic core invites geometricization and contact with
neighboring duality theories. The following conjectures make this precise.

\subsection{Francis--Gaitsgory as the commutator shadow}

The associative story developed here is not the Francis--Gaitsgory Lie/cocommutative story.
Rather, the latter should appear as the commutative shadow of the former.

\begin{theorem}[Comm\-utator-shadow theorem]
\ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere
\label{thm:FG-shadow}
Let $A$ be a filtered strongly admissible associative chiral algebra with exhaustive separated
commutator filtration $F_{\mathrm{com}}^\bullet A$. Assume:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
\item $\gr_{\mathrm{com}}A$ is $E_\infty$-chiral;
\item $\gr_{\mathrm{com}}A$ is quadratic Koszul on the commutative/Lie side;
\item the induced filtration on $\Barch(A)$ is complete and strongly convergent.
\end{enumerate}
Then there is a convergent spectral sequence

import re, subprocess, os, shlex
labels = [
'ch:bv-brst','ch:feynman','comp:bp-kappa-three-paths','comp:burns-koszul-datum','comp:superstring-ghost-koszul','comp:virasoro-m3','comp:virasoro-m4','conj:bar-cobar-path-integral-heisenberg','conj:bar-cobar-path-integral','conj:bar-graph-complex','conj:bar-topological-brst','conj:bar-worldline','conj:bphz-recursion','conj:disk-local-perturbative-fm'
]
paths = ['main.tex','chapters','appendices','standalone']
for lab in labels:
    print('LABEL', lab)
    cmd = \"rg -n --no-heading --glob '*.tex' '{}' {}\".format(re.escape(lab), ' '.join(shlex.quote(p) for p in paths))
    try:
        out = subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        out = e.output
    print(out)

\label{part:physics-bridges}

\noindent
The kernel $d\log(z_i - z_j)$ is the Cauchy kernel on a curve, the
Beilinson--Drinfeld chiral propagator, the Green's function of the
$\bar\partial$-operator, and the Kontsevich configuration-space
integrand. The Arnold relation $\eta_{12} \wedge \eta_{23}
+ \eta_{23} \wedge \eta_{31} + \eta_{31} \wedge \eta_{12} = 0$ is
simultaneously the braid relation, the Jacobi identity, the
Yang--Baxter equation at $u = 0$, and the BRST Ward identity for
triple collisions. The categorical logarithm therefore has
incarnations in every field that uses propagators on configuration
spaces: its bar differential is the worldsheet BRST differential
(Chapter~\ref{ch:bv-brst}); its genus tower is the string
perturbation series; its graph expansion produces Kontsevich weights
and Vassiliev invariants; at critical level $k = -h^\vee$ it
recovers the algebra of opers and enters the geometric Langlands
programme.

One kernel. One relation. Many incarnations.
 succeeded in 51ms:

The bar complex looks like a Feynman expansion. The differential
is a sum over trees weighted by propagators $\eta_{ij} = d\log(z_i - z_j)$,
the degree counts vertices, and iterated residues on
Fulton--MacPherson boundary strata play the role of UV divergences.
The similarity is striking enough to demand an answer: is the bar
complex a literal reformulation of perturbative QFT, or only a
structural analogy?

The honest answer distinguishes two regimes. For the Heisenberg
algebra $\mathcal{H}_{\kappa^{\mathrm{Heis}}}$, the bar complex
reproduces the free-boson residue calculus exactly, and
Conjecture~\ref{conj:bar-cobar-path-integral-heisenberg} records the
expected all-genera path-integral identification. For interacting
theories, Volume~I proves only the
genus-$0$ algebraic BRST/bar comparison on the Kac--Moody locus and
the local binary/ternary reduction package; the general perturbative
comparison remains conjectural
\textup{(}Conjecture~\ref{conj:bar-cobar-path-integral}\textup{)}.
The Feynman--bar mnemonic (Remark~\ref{rem:feynman-bar-bridge})
 succeeded in 51ms:
Total output lines: 871

The bar-cobar construction on a curve~$X$ is a chiral analogue of
Kontsevich formality~\cite{Kon99}: polyvector fields become chiral
algebras, the upper half-plane becomes~$X$, angle forms $d\varphi$
become logarithmic forms~$\eta$, and the $L_\infty$
quasi-isomorphism becomes bar-cobar duality. The formality morphism
$\mathcal{F}\colon H^*(\barB^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \to \cA$ sums over
Feynman graphs with configuration space integral weights.
\end{remark}

\begin{conjecture}[Disk-local perturbative/FM comparison;
 \ClaimStatusConjectured]\label{conj:disk-local-perturbative-fm}
Let $U \subset X$ be a holomorphic disk chart on a curve, and suppose a
holomorphic-topological perturbative field theory on~$U$ produces the
same local chiral algebra model $\cA|_U$.\linebreak[1]
Then the regularized perturbative BRST-anomaly brackets
of Gaiotto--Kulp--Wu~\cite{GKW24} agree, on that disk chart, with
the local bar operations cut out by the Fulton--MacPherson collision

@@ -349,3 +349,3 @@
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
@@ -482,3 +482,3 @@
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}

index 7df188126147dbfe9c77ab9492356d703461218e..a64310cdb2031f2f0e6bcb474baf2c994b472232
--- a/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
+++ b/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}

index 7df188126147dbfe9c77ab9492356d703461218e..a64310cdb2031f2f0e6bcb474baf2c994b472232
--- a/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
+++ b/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}

**Updating references and labels**

I’m thinking it’s time to patch up any missing references, like comp:bp, which seems to be just phantom. It looks like I don't have any other references to update. I also need to address the leftover old labels in live surfaces due to some name changes. I think I’ll use grep to limit the search to the live text surfaces for these old labels. That should help clean things up!
codex
The main label surgery is in. I’m now doing the hostile re-read pass: grep the live TeX surface for any stale old labels or refs in Vol I, then I’ll decide whether `comp:heisenberg-center` can be truthfully fixed or needs to be marked artifact-blocked.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import re, subprocess, shlex
labels = [
'ch:bv-brst','ch:feynman','comp:bp-kappa-three-paths','comp:burns-koszul-datum','comp:superstring-ghost-koszul','comp:virasoro-m3','comp:virasoro-m4','conj:bar-cobar-path-integral-heisenberg','conj:bar-cobar-path-integral','conj:bar-graph-complex','conj:bar-topological-brst','conj:bar-worldline','conj:bphz-recursion','conj:disk-local-perturbative-fm'
]
paths = ['main.tex','chapters','appendices','standalone']
for lab in labels:
    cmd = \"rg -n --no-heading --glob '*.tex' '{}' {}\".format(re.escape(lab), ' '.join(shlex.quote(p) for p in paths))
    try:
        out = subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        out = e.output
    if out.strip():
        print('LABEL', lab)
        print(out)

@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex

@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}

 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex

@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}

 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Higher genus corrections}

 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}

@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Higher genus corrections}

I’m thinking maybe the comp:heisenberg-center is still a duplicate, and it might be worth fixing, although the user only requested the original first 20. I should check the actual sources of these duplicates, focusing on the artifacts causing issues. Moving backups could help too!
thinking
**Considering file edits and duplicates**

The user mentioned only to edit assigned files, so I need to tread carefully. I could patch the backup label to `comp:v1-heisenberg-center-backup` and delete the .aux file. Since .aux is generated, deleting it shouldn't be harmful—yes, it seems safe to delete. However, if the backup file is user data, renaming that label may suffice. I want to ensure I’m handling files correctly, especially since the duplicates are still showing up, so a fix here is important!
thinking
**Assessing file edits and backups**

I’m considering patching the backup label and deleting the .aux file, but the user might prefer to keep the backup intact. Perhaps I could just move the backup out of the chapters instead. Renaming the label within the backup seems like a minimal and less destructive option. For the .aux file, I could delete it using apply_patch since it’s untracked. I wonder if I need PE-7 for the backup label—maybe it's not necessary after all!

@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Higher genus corrections}

diff --git a/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex b/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
index 7df188126147dbfe9c77ab9492356d703461218e..a64310cdb2031f2f0e6bcb474baf2c994b472232
--- a/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
+++ b/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

index f218b5b54a55c61c080a0a8ac1d2082c17587942..4de065a89f850d6a84b354d1f7e9b16c36aec04a

 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams]\label{sec:feynman_genus_g}
 The Feynman diagram interpretation of the bar-cobar complex (the identification
 of bar chains with off-shell amplitudes and cobar chains with on-shell propagator
-templates) is developed in Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}.
+templates) is developed in Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}.
 \end{remark}
 
-% Feynman diagram exposition (formerly ~210 lines) moved to Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}.
+% Feynman diagram exposition (formerly ~210 lines) moved to Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}.

diff --git a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}

index 7df188126147dbfe9c77ab9492356d703461218e..a64310cdb2031f2f0e6bcb474baf2c994b472232
--- a/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
+++ b/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex
@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Higher genus corrections}

import re, subprocess, shlex
labels = [
'ch:bv-brst','ch:feynman','comp:bp-kappa-three-paths','comp:burns-koszul-datum','comp:superstring-ghost-koszul','comp:virasoro-m3','comp:virasoro-m4','conj:bar-cobar-path-integral-heisenberg','conj:bar-cobar-path-integral','conj:bar-graph-complex','conj:bar-topological-brst','conj:bar-worldline','conj:bphz-recursion','conj:disk-local-perturbative-fm','comp:heisenberg-center'
]
paths = ['main.tex','chapters','appendices','standalone']
for lab in labels:
    cmd = \"rg -n --no-heading --glob '*.tex' '{}' {}\".format(re.escape(lab), ' '.join(shlex.quote(p) for p in paths))
    try:
        out = subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        out = e.output
    if out.strip():
        print('LABEL', lab)
        print(out)

-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Higher genus corrections}

--- a/appendices/spectral_higher_genus.tex

@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

 ladder. Level~(C) is a physical realization of~(B) and is

--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex

@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Higher genus corrections}

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess, re, shlex
labels = [
'ch:v1-bv-brst','ch:v1-feynman','comp:v1-bp-kappa-three-paths','comp:v1-burns-koszul-datum','comp:v1-superstring-ghost-koszul','comp:v1-virasoro-m3','comp:v1-virasoro-m4','conj:v1-bar-cobar-path-integral-heisenberg','conj:v1-bar-cobar-path-integral','conj:v1-bar-graph-complex','conj:v1-bar-topological-brst','conj:v1-bar-worldline','conj:v1-bphz-recursion','conj:v1-disk-local-perturbative-fm','comp:v1-heisenberg-center'
]
for lab in labels:
    cmd = f\"find main.tex chapters appendices standalone -type f \\( -name '*.tex' -o -name '*.bak*' \\) -print0 | xargs -0 grep -Hn '\\\\\\\\label{{{lab}}}' 2>/dev/null\"
    try:
        out = subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        out = e.output
    cnt = len([ln for ln in out.splitlines() if ln.strip()])
    print(f'{lab}\\t{cnt}')
    if out.strip():
        print(out.strip())

@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@
 
 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}

 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}
