diff --git a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Higher genus corrections}

thinking
**Evaluating chapter references**

I’m considering whether we should remove or rename phantoms to something like `stub:` labels. If chapters are included in the current build, do I still need phantoms? It seems the main phantoms are necessary only when chapters are omitted. It looks like physics chapters might be moved to Volume II and omitted from the main build. So, if the user's command only scans chapter files, then phantoms aren’t relevant. I think I understand the references better now.
thinking
**Verifying phantoms and labels**

I could mention that phantoms are intentional, so maybe there's no need to change them. I might need to verify if the global duplicate command still lists fixed labels. Perhaps running a targeted grep for blocked labels would help confirm things. It seems I should also track the fix status mapping. I can script a summary of the first 20 original labels and check if they're still duplicates. Let’s work on a small script to confirm which fixed ones aren't in the current duplicate set!
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess, re
orig = [
'ch:bv-brst','ch:feynman','ch:k3-times-e','ch:ordered-associative-chiral-kd','comp:bp-kappa-three-paths','comp:bulk-wn','comp:burns-koszul-datum','comp:heisenberg-center','comp:superstring-ghost-koszul','comp:virasoro-m3','comp:virasoro-m4','conj:agt-bar-cobar','conj:bar-cobar-path-integral-heisenberg','conj:bar-cobar-path-integral','conj:bar-graph-complex','conj:bar-topological-brst','conj:bar-worldline','conj:bordered','conj:bphz-recursion','conj:disk-local-perturbative-fm']
current = subprocess.check_output(\"(grep -roh '\\\\\\\\label{["'^}]*}'"' ~/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/ ~/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2/chapters/ ~/calabi-yau-quantum-groups/chapters/ 2>/dev/null) | sort | uniq -d\", shell=True, text=True)
current = {re.search(r'\\\\label\\{(["'^}]*)'"\\}', line).group(1) for line in current.splitlines() if line.strip()}
for lab in orig:
    print(f'{lab}\\t{\"DUPLICATE\" if lab in current else \"CLEARED\"}')

+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
index 8b72abc4d8d1ebf87433d0b8194687561328dc69..ba301a61d55c0035bab6db4d7a1b6988afe1545b
--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex
@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Higher genus corrections}

 succeeded in 52ms:
The bar-cobar construction on a curve~$X$ is a chiral analogue of
Kontsevich formality~\cite{Kon99}: polyvector fields become chiral
algebras, the upper half-plane becomes~$X$, angle forms $d\varphi$
become logarithmic forms~$\eta$, and the $L_\infty$
quasi-isomorphism becomes bar-cobar duality. The formality morphism
$\mathcal{F}\colon H^*(\barB^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \to \cA$ sums over
Feynman graphs with configuration space integral weights.
\end{remark}

\begin{conjecture}[Disk-local perturbative/FM comparison;
 \ClaimStatusConjectured]\label{conj:v1-disk-local-perturbative-fm}
Let $U \subset X$ be a holomorphic disk chart on a curve, and suppose a
holomorphic-topological perturbative field theory on~$U$ produces the
same local chiral algebra model $\cA|_U$.\linebreak[1]
Then the regularized perturbative BRST-anomaly brackets
of Gaiotto--Kulp--Wu~\cite{GKW24} agree, on that disk chart, with
the local bar operations cut out by the Fulton--MacPherson collision
strata:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item the binary perturbative bracket agrees with the bar residue/OPE
 succeeded in 52ms:

The bar complex looks like a Feynman expansion. The differential
is a sum over trees weighted by propagators $\eta_{ij} = d\log(z_i - z_j)$,
the degree counts vertices, and iterated residues on
Fulton--MacPherson boundary strata play the role of UV divergences.
The similarity raises a precise question: is the bar
complex a literal reformulation of perturbative QFT, or only a
structural analogy?

The honest answer distinguishes two regimes. For the Heisenberg
algebra $\mathcal{H}_{\kappa^{\mathrm{Heis}}}$, the bar complex
reproduces the free-boson residue calculus exactly, and
Conjecture~\ref{conj:v1-bar-cobar-path-integral-heisenberg} records the
expected all-genera path-integral identification. For interacting
theories, Volume~I proves only the
genus-$0$ algebraic BRST/bar comparison on the Kac--Moody locus and
the local binary/ternary reduction package; the general perturbative
comparison remains conjectural
\textup{(}Conjecture~\ref{conj:v1-bar-cobar-path-integral}\textup{)}.
The Feynman--bar mnemonic (Remark~\ref{rem:feynman-bar-bridge})

 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

--- a/chapters/examples/yangians_foundations.tex
+++ b/chapters/examples/yangians_foundations.tex
@@ -79,7 +79,7 @@
 Level~(B), developed in this chapter, geometrizes the spectral
 parameter and produces the $\Eone$-bar-cobar duality and the DK
 ladder. Level~(C) is a physical realization of~(B) and is
-discussed in Chapter~\ref{ch:bv-brst}.
+discussed in Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-bv-brst}.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four-level structure: Yangians]

index b24916bc82195d112d0306fbec56dee8a06cb1c8..35268cd9afb7fcc164fc52b08126d5c3c4c7ef9e
--- a/chapters/frame/heisenberg_frame.tex.bak.abelian_cs_fix
+++ b/chapters/frame/heisenberg_frame.tex.bak.abelian_cs_fix
@@ -3754,7 +3754,7 @@
 reduces to a transparent computation.

@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}

**Evaluating labels and potential issues**

I’ve spot-checked okay for potential issues with main phantoms. It seems like there might be problems when counts of new labels in both main and chapters exceed one. Since the user asked about checking \ref and \label validity, I verified with grep that the old labels have disappeared from live TeX surfaces. However, we'll have to mention that some phantoms in `main.tex` remain as deliberate stubs. I have to note that not everything was build-tested and we're limited by read-only access outside writable root.
thinking
**Detailing cross-volume keeper decisions**

I need to mention the cross-volume decisions regarding the labels kept for different volumes. For Volume II, notable labels include `ch:bv-brst`, `ch:feynman`, and several composite and conjectural entries, with some potentially blocked. Volume I has labels like `ch:ordered-associative-chiral-kd` and `conj:bordered`, while Volume III retains `ch:k3-times-e`. I should clarify that some were not built and consider presenting a concise flat list, showing paths and line references to help clarify status. Through rerunning the original duplicate grep, I found 15 of the original 20 cleared, but five remain blocked.
thinking
**Clarifying duplicate definitions**

I’ve noticed that when counts show duplicate definitions in both the main and chapter files, this doesn’t impact the requested duplicate grep since it only scans `chapters/`. Those entries are compile stubs located under `\ifannalsedition`. However, should I mention this situation? I think it might be unnecessary, but if the user later questions why new labels are counted twice, clarity will be essential for them.

 \begin{remark}[Spectral sequence and genus expansion]\label{rem:ss-genus-spectral}
 %: primary label rem:ss-genus lives in higher_genus_foundations.tex
-Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}).
+Heuristically, the spectral sequence page $E_r$ captures graphs with at most $r-1$ loops: $E_1$ is tree-level (genus~$0$), $E_2$ includes one-loop corrections (genus~$1$), and $E_\infty$ subsumes all genera. This analogy is suggestive but not a theorem; the precise page-genus dictionary requires graph complex technology (Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}).
 
 The differentials $d_r \colon E_r^{p,q} \to E_r^{p+r,q-r+1}$ are computed by integrating correlators over moduli of curves, but the identification of $d_r$ with genus-$(r-1)$ contributions is not established in general. See Remark~\ref{rem:ss-genus-scope} for the scope of what is proved.
 \end{remark}

--- a/chapters/theory/nilpotent_completion.tex

@@ -347,7 +347,7 @@
 \subsection{Feynman diagram expansion}
 
 \begin{remark}[Feynman diagrams and conilpotent degree]\label{rem:feynman-conilpotent}
-The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
+The conilpotent filtration levels $F^n/F^{n+1}$ correspond, under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, to graphs with exactly~$n$ internal edges. In particular: $F^0/F^1$ consists of vacuum contributions (no internal edges), $F^1/F^2$ of tree-level propagators (single internal edge), and $F^n/F^{n+1}$ of diagrams with~$n$ internal edges. The symmetry factors arising in the perturbative expansion are the orders of the automorphism groups of the corresponding graphs. The completion $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\mathcal{A}) = \varprojlim_n \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n$ amounts to summing over all graph topologies, a formal power series that requires the $I$-adic topology to converge.
 \end{remark}
 
 \section{Explicit examples}
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 \end{theorem}
 
 \begin{remark}[Physical interpretation]
-Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
+Under the Feynman diagram identification of Chapter~\ref{ch:v1-feynman}, the $I$-adic completion topology is the algebraic counterpart of the loop expansion: convergence of the inverse system $\{\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})/I^n\}$ ensures that the formal sum over all loop orders defines a well-defined dg coalgebra. The analogy with renormalization group flow is suggestive (both organize quantum corrections by a filtration parameter), but the precise relationship requires the graph complex technology developed in that chapter.
 \end{remark}
